# UC-17.1.80 · ISE ANC Closed-Loop Effectiveness — Quarantine-to-Compromise-Stop Time

> **Criticality:** High &middot; **Difficulty:** Advanced &middot; **Pillar:** Security &middot; **Type:** Security, Compliance &middot; **Wave:** Run &middot; **Status:** Verified

*ISE can quarantine a misbehaving device. We measure if the bad activity actually stops after quarantine — sometimes the quarantine doesn't take effect, and bad traffic continues.*

---

## Description

Measures whether ANC quarantine actually stops the malicious activity. Correlates ANC timestamp with the last observed malicious event — if attacks continue post-ANC, the closed loop is broken.

## Value

ANC is only as good as the network enforcement that follows. If switches don't enforce CoA, malicious activity continues — ANC becomes theatre. This UC measures the actual security outcome, not just the ANC API call.

## Implementation

Schedule every 30 min.

## Control test

### Positive scenario

Quarantine an endpoint actively attacking. Measure time until malicious traffic stops. Should be <2 minutes; otherwise UC-17.1.80 fires.

### Negative scenario

Quarantine immediately stops attack. Healthy KPI.

## Detailed Implementation

Standard pattern.

## SPL

```spl
index=nac sourcetype="cisco:ise:syslog" MessageCode=86001 anc_action=quarantine
| eval anc_time=_time, endpoint_mac=mac
| join endpoint_mac [search index=notable severity=high earliest=-1d | rename _time as last_attack_time]
| eval quarantine_to_attack_stop=anc_time - last_attack_time
| stats avg(quarantine_to_attack_stop) as avg_seconds, count by anc_policy
```

## Visualization

Quarantine-to-stop time gauge; per-endpoint timeline; failed-quarantine list.

## Known False Positives

- Endpoint moved to a different switch port — CoA didn't propagate. Operations issue.
- Endpoint legitimately back on network after admin unquarantine.

## References

- [Cisco ISE 3.4 — Adaptive Network Control](https://www.cisco.com/c/en/us/)
